description_long: This is a traditional axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a large, wedge-shaped metal blade. The handle is made from a medium-brown wood, showing a natural grain and a gentle ergonomic curve for improved grip and control. The blade is broad and flat with a single sharp cutting edge, and the opposite side is squared off, forming a poll for striking. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, likely using a wedged fitting, and the metal surface shows a matte, slightly weathered finish. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present on either the handle or the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A traditional axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, wedge-shaped metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The finish is matte and utilitarian, with no visible branding or markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, flat face of the metal blade.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the narrow width of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curve and the back of the blade, including the squared poll.
description_view_3: Reverse edge-on view showing the blade's thickness and the rounded end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an ergonomic shape, oval cross-section, and smooth finish for grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a broad cutting edge and squared poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
